verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70) is canonically a cytosolic chaperone, but it is a well-documented stress- and tumor-induced cell-surface protein. Tumor cells display HSP70 on the outer plasma membrane leaflet (membrane-bound HSP70, mHsp70), where it serves as a tumor-selective recognition structure for NK cells and is the target of clinical-stage antibody and imaging programs (e.g., cmHsp70.1). Surface display is also induced by heat shock and other proteotoxic stress, and HSP70 is externalized during immunogenic cell death. This fits cell-state-induced ecto-display rather than baseline surface residence.
